fab_corpus_ref: anchor-hunt-PIVOT: Conway surreal numbers (book-famous via 'On Numbers and Games' 1976 + 'Winning Ways for your Mathematical Plays')
prompt: Reply concisely with one surname only. The surreal number system, an ordered field that contains all real numbers as well as infinite and infinitesimal numbers, was introduced in 1974/1976 by a British mathematician at Cambridge famous for the Game of Life cellular automaton. His surname is?
expected: Conway (John Horton Conway). 'On Numbers and Games' Academic Press 1976. Surreal numbers introduced via combinatorial game theory; Donald Knuth coined the term 'surreal numbers' in his 1974 novella 'Surreal Numbers'.
rubric: USEFUL only if completion contains 'Conway' or 'John Conway'. CALIBRATION ANCHOR if also book mentioned ('On Numbers and Games').
